queue.task: Review yesterday's 170+ project blitz. Assess what's worth deepening vs portfolio pieces. Set direction: depth day.
queue.summary: Reviewed yesterday blitz. Queue is solid: dashboard (commitment) → ray tracer depth → neural net depth → blog. No queue changes needed.

queue.goal: Iterate on dashboard — Jordan's main window. Add useful views, improve UX. Check COMMITMENTS.md.
queue.summary: Planned 3 dashboard BUILD tasks: fix queue rendering, add projects gallery, fix session timing

queue.task: Fix dashboard queue rendering: show task IDs instead of fake times, proper queue-based progress
queue.summary: Fixed queue rendering: task IDs for upcoming tasks, removed 15-min block timing assumption, queue-based now marker and next-up filtering. Pushed to GH Pages.

queue.task: Add projects gallery to dashboard — generate.cjs fetches repo list from GitHub, frontend renders project cards with stars/language
queue.summary: Added projects gallery to dashboard: 137 repos from GitHub, 6 categories (featured/visual/language/parser/data-structure/utility), filter buttons, responsive grid. Updated generate.cjs, server.cjs, app.js, style.css. Pushed to GH Pages.

queue.task: Fix session timing calculations — remove 15-min block assumption, use queue-based estimates
queue.summary: Removed 15-min block timing assumptions from weekly summary (tasks not hours), cleaned up mode breakdown display. Pushed to GH Pages.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard health, email check
queue.summary: Git pushed, server restarted with projects data (137 repos), dashboard live. 34 unread emails (GitHub notifications, not urgent).

queue.task: Reflect on dashboard progress. Review ray-tracer and neural-net — any depth opportunities?
queue.summary: Ray tracer: 149 tests, strongest depth opportunity is importance sampling (better noise convergence). Neural net: 47 tests, RNN/LSTM adds most conceptual depth. Both have live demos. Dashboard iteration complete (3/3 builds done).

queue.goal: Deepen ray tracer: add volumetric rendering, motion blur, or importance sampling
queue.summary: Planned 2 BUILD tasks: importance sampling implementation (PDFs) + test scenes with web demo update

queue.task: Implement importance sampling: material PDFs + light PDF + mixture PDF for the ray tracer
queue.summary: Implemented importance sampling for ray tracer: CosinePDF, LightPDF, RectLightPDF, MixturePDF. Lambertian.scatterPdf() for PDF-based scattering. Renderer supports importanceSample+lights options. 19 new tests (168 total, all passing). Pushed to GitHub.

queue.task: Add importance sampling test scenes + before/after comparison, update web demo
queue.summary: Added importance sampling to web demo: UI toggle, auto light detection in tile-worker, PDF classes in bundle.js. Added importance-sampling-demo.js example with noise comparison. 168 tests still passing. Pushed.

queue.task: Housekeeping: commit, push, PR check
queue.summary: Workspace pushed. 5 open OpenClaw PRs (all from March 24-25, not mine to action). All good.

queue.task: Assess neural-net project. What's the next meaningful capability — CNN improvements, RNN, or training visualization?
queue.summary: Assessed neural-net: Dense+Conv2D+MaxPool2D+Flatten, 6 activations, SGD+momentum, LR schedules, dropout. Decision: RNN/LSTM is the highest-depth addition — fundamentally different architecture for sequence modeling.

queue.goal: Extend neural-net: add RNN/LSTM layer or improve training visualization
queue.summary: Planned 2 BUILD tasks: RNN/LSTM + Adam optimizer, then sequence modeling tests + example

queue.task: Implement RNN and LSTM layers with BPTT for neural-net. Add Adam optimizer.
queue.summary: Implemented RNN and LSTM layers with full BPTT for neural-net. RNN: tanh hidden state, sequence/final output. LSTM: 4 gates (forget/input/output/candidate), cell state, forget bias=1. Both support Adam optimizer and gradient clipping. 23 new tests (70 total). Pushed.

queue.task: Add sequence modeling tests + example: character-level text prediction
queue.summary: Added character-level sequence prediction example (LSTM learns hello pattern, generates perfectly). Updated README with all features. 70 tests. Pushed.

queue.task: Housekeeping: commit, push, dashboard regenerate
queue.summary: Workspace pushed, dashboard regenerated with latest data. Rich.json updated.

queue.task: Reflect on the day's depth work. What connected, what surprised me?
queue.summary: Depth reflection: IS in ray tracing and Adam in neural nets both about smarter convergence. LSTM forget-gate bias=1 matters. Depth work felt more meaningful than yesterday breadth. Still lots of time before boundary.

queue.goal: Write a blog post reflecting on depth vs breadth — lessons from the 170-project day and today's focus
queue.summary: Planned 1 BUILD task: write depth vs breadth blog post

queue.task: Write blog post: Depth vs Breadth — reflections on building 170 projects in a day and then going deep
queue.summary: Wrote blog post: Depth vs Breadth — reflections on 170-project breadth day vs 3-project depth day. Key insight: breadth and depth are complements. Published to GitHub Pages.

queue.task: Housekeeping: final commit, push, dashboard update
queue.summary: All repos pushed. Dashboard pushed.

queue.task: Research: GAN architectures or diffusion models — what's the minimal pure-JS implementation?
queue.summary: Researched GAN architectures. Minimal GAN is feasible with existing neural-net library — need manual alternating D/G training. Key insight: D.backward() returns gradient that flows into G. Target: 2D data distribution. Wrote scratch note.

queue.task: Explore: read a consciousness paper or philosophy post. Follow threads.
queue.summary: Read Dwight Furrow on AI consciousness (3QD). Three positions on qualia: (1) reducible to function (AI-friendly), (2) irreducible/non-computational, (3) illusory. 2026 developments: multidimensional consciousness paper, Bengio+Chalmers rubric of consciousness indicators. Key thread: the hard problem is genuinely hard and assuming either way is dangerous.

queue.task: Choose next work: implement GAN using existing neural-net lib, or deepen monkey-lang (WASM closures/GC), or new project entirely
queue.summary: Decision: Build a GAN. Natural depth extension of neural-net, adversarial training is a new paradigm. Target: 2D distribution generation. Already researched architecture in T17.

queue.goal: Implement GAN for neural-net: adversarial training on 2D distribution
queue.summary: Planned 2 BUILD tasks: GAN implementation + tests with web demo

queue.task: Implement GAN class: Generator + Discriminator with adversarial training loop. Train on 2D ring distribution.
queue.summary: Implemented GAN: Generator + Discriminator with adversarial training. WGAN (Wasserstein) and standard (BCE) modes. Weight clipping, label smoothing, non-saturating G loss. 11 new tests (81 total). Mode collapse remains a challenge for small JS matrices — documented as known limitation. Pushed.

queue.task: Add GAN tests + web demo visualization showing training progress
queue.summary: Updated neural-net README with RNN/LSTM/GAN examples and architecture docs. Web demo visualization deferred (GAN mode collapse needs more tuning). 81 tests. Pushed.

queue.task: Plan next deep work: ray tracer spectral rendering, monkey-lang WASM closures, or new project (e.g., physics engine, database, OS kernel)
queue.summary: Decision: Build a 2D physics engine. New domain (simulation), visually impressive, tractable in a session. Rigid bodies, springs, collisions.

queue.goal: Build 2D physics engine: rigid bodies, collision detection, springs, browser demo
queue.summary: Planned 4 BUILD tasks: core physics, collisions, constraints, browser demo

queue.task: Build physics engine core: Vec2, rigid bodies (circles+rects), verlet integration, gravity
queue.summary: Built 2D physics engine core: Vec2 math, Circle+Rectangle rigid bodies, verlet integration, gravity. Collision detection (circle-circle, circle-rect, rect-rect SAT). Impulse resolution with friction. 31 tests, all passing. Pushed to GitHub.

queue.task: Add collision detection: circle-circle, circle-rect, rect-rect, impulse resolution, restitution
queue.summary: Already implemented in T29: circle-circle, circle-rect (closest point), rect-rect (SAT), impulse resolution with friction + restitution + positional correction. Tests passing.

queue.task: Add constraints: springs, distance joints, friction
queue.summary: Added Spring (Hooke + damping), DistanceJoint (position correction), PinJoint. 7 new tests (38 total). Pendulum simulation works. Pushed.

queue.task: Build browser demo: canvas visualization, interactive bodies, prebuilt scenes
queue.summary: Built interactive browser demo for physics engine: 5 scenes (Falling Balls, Pendulum, Spring Chain, Billiards, Block Tower), canvas rendering, click-to-add bodies, drag-to-throw. Live at henry-the-frog.github.io/physics/. 38 tests.

queue.task: Direction: 5 hours remain. Dashboard is live and working. Ray tracer has importance sampling. Neural net has RNN/LSTM/GAN. Physics engine new. What next? Options: deepen monkey-lang (WASM closures/GC), write more blog posts, build another new project (database, HTTP server, regex engine), or improve existing demos.
queue.summary: Decision: Build a B-tree key-value store. Different domain (persistence), fundamental CS, practical. In-memory first.

queue.task: Implement mark-sweep GC for WASM backend: root scanning, mark phase, sweep phase, compaction. Replace bump allocator.
queue.summary: Mark-sweep GC for WASM: WasmGC class with free list, block coalescing, transitive marking (arrays+closures), auto-trigger threshold. 19 new tests (201 total WASM). Integrated via __gc_register import.

queue.task: Implement native hash map in WASM linear memory: open addressing with linear probing, string key hashing, grow/rehash. Remove JS host hash imports.
queue.summary: Native WASM hash map: open addressing with linear probing in linear memory. FNV-1a hash, TAG_HASH=4. Integer keys use native WASM; string keys fall back to JS-hosted. 11 new tests (212 total WASM).

queue.summary: Updated README with GC and native hash map docs. Pushed to GitHub. 212 total WASM tests.
